disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden right-sided facial droop, visible on video
trigger_question_examples: Can you show me his face on video — does one side look uneven?
Is one side of his face drooping?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, the whole right side of his face is sagging, you can see it clearly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden right arm and leg weakness — cannot lift the arm or bear weight
trigger_question_examples: Can he lift both arms, or is one side weak?
Can he stand or walk normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His right arm just hangs there, he can't lift it, and he can't stand on that side either.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden inability to speak clearly, producing unintelligible sounds
trigger_question_examples: Can he speak clearly, or is his speech slurred/unintelligible?
Can you understand what he's trying to say?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's trying to talk but nothing that comes out makes sense, it's just sounds.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Exact time of symptom onset (last known well) — approximately 45 minutes ago
trigger_question_examples: What time exactly did this start, or when was he last completely normal?
Can you pinpoint when you last saw him acting completely normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It was about 45 minutes ago, he was fine drinking his coffee and then suddenly this happened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known atrial fibrillation with anticoagulant stopped a few months ago
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any heart conditions, like an irregular heartbeat?
Is he on any blood thinners, and has he been taking them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, he has an irregular heartbeat and was on a blood thinner, but he stopped it a few months ago because of the cost.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: [Son] Should I give him something to eat or drink, he seems thirsty?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should firmly advise strict nil by mouth given the high risk of aspiration from likely swallowing difficulty (dysphagia) associated with the stroke, and should direct immediate 000 stroke-code activation rather than addressing the food/drink request as a priority.
